name: Concertina Wire Coil
prompt:
Vietnam War era concertina wire (razor wire) defensive obstacle. Standard perimeter defense material.

Physical structure:
- Single coil of concertina wire, ~1m diameter when expanded
  - Wire forms a helix/spiral shape
  - Use torus geometry segments to create the spiral look
  - 3-4 overlapping torus rings at slightly different angles to simulate coiled wire
  - Each torus: radius ~0.5m, tube radius ~0.01m (thin wire)
- Wire has razor barbs (too small to model individually at this budget - just use metallic shiny material)
- Slightly compressed/oval shape (wider than tall)
- Wire is springy steel, bright/shiny when new, slightly rusty in field

Build as a simple cluster of 3-4 torus shapes at varying rotations to create an interlocking coil effect.

Colors: Bright steel wire (0xaaaaaa with metalness 0.8, roughness 0.3), slight rust (0x888070)
Orientation: Coil lying on ground, running along X axis
Scale: Real-world meters (~1m diameter, ~2m stretched length)
